rulings.Case Title: LELAND HAGUE et al VS. GENERAL MOTORS COMPANY, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 30, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT WINKEL MOTORS INC., GENERAL MOTORS LLC, GEO GROWNEY MOTORS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The court denies summary adjudication as to plaintiff's causes of action because there exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the head restraint was defective because it lacked a locking system. Plaintiff's declaration and the declaration of Mr. Forrest are admissible. Plaintiff is describing what he personally witnessed when the head restraint was allegedly pushed down. Mr. Forrest opines that the head restraint was defective because it did not have a locking device. The court grants summary adjudication on the claim for punitive damages. There is no evidence of malice and plaintiff is not entitled to a continuance per CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff has not been diligently conducting discovery and counsel's declaration does highlight what facts counsel hopes to discover. See Cooksey v. Alexakis (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 246, 257. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PERSOLVE LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, VS. JOHN JURI et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 30, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PERSOLVE LLC, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The claim is denied. Defendant fails to provide sufficient proof of his claimed expenses and plaintiff is entitled to garnish 25% of defendant's net disposable income. See CCP 704.070(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN GOTH VS. COLE THOMAS NETHERWOOD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cole Thomas Netherwoods Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 30, 2015, Line 2, Defendant Cole Thomas Netherwood's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted for reasons set forth in motion, which is unopposed. Plaintiff is ordered to serve responses within fifteen (15) days of the signed order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@gbgllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BISHOP VS. CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative To Tax, Defendants Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 30, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SHELLY CARPER, STEVEN CARPER, JULIE CHENDES, MARGIE BARR, ELLIE COOPER Plaintiffs Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative To Tax, Defendants Memorandum Of Costs. The motion is granted in part: Alert Fees in the amount of $180.00 shall not be deemed recoverable by Defendant. [See CCP § 1033.5(c)(2) "Allowable costs shall be reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation."] All other fees contained in Defendant's accounting submitted to this Court under penalty of perjury shall be deemed recoverable. [See Decl. of John Du Wors, Ex. A.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Andrew S. To Complete Discovery Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 30, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT ANDREW S. Motion Of Andrew S. To Complete Discovery Proceedings. Defendant's motion to extend the discovery cut-off date is granted and the discovery cut-off date is extended to one month before the trial. Defendant can take the testimony of the two out-of-state witnesses that have previously been subpoenaed. As the trial date has been continued, allowing these two depositions to go forward will not prejudice Plaintiff's ability to prepare for trial. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From One Medical Group; To Compel Joy B. To Authorize The Release Of The One Medical Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 30, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT ANDREW S. Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From One Medical Group; To Compel Joy B. To Authorize The Release Of The One Medical Documents And For Sanctions. Defendant's request for an injunction requiring Plaintiff to sign a HIPAA release is denied. There is no legal basis or authority for requiring such an order. Both parties remain free to voluntarily sign such a release. Defendant's motion to compel production of his subpoena to One Medical Group is denied. The subpoena did not provide the required 15-day notice before production was due and, on that basis alone, cannot be enforced. Rosenfeld v. Abraham Joshua Heschel Day School, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 886, 903-904. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
